questionText: Choose the correct result for this puppy treat puzzle. Treats are packed in bags of 7. There are more than 30 treats and fewer than 50 treats. The total is a multiple of 7, but it is not 42. One more clue: the digits of the total add to 7. Which total fits all the clues?
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can identify multiples of a given number
issues: [concept_accuracy, explanation_quality, clarity] | The item has no correct answer: among multiples of 7 between 30 and 50, 35 has digit sum 8, 42 is excluded, and 49 has digit sum 13, so none satisfy all clues. | The explanation explicitly admits the mismatch, confirming the concept/correct-answer error. | For a grade 4 MCQ, an item with no valid option is not usable as written. | Fix: Revise one clue so exactly one option works, such as changing the digit-sum clue to 'the digits add to 13' so 49 is correct. | Fix: Alternatively replace one option with a valid total that matches all clues and update the explanation accordingly. | Fix: Rewrite the explanation so it cleanly verifies the single correct option without contradiction.
